name: Vision Language Models: A Survey of 26K Papers (CVPR, ICLR
snippet: Takeaways for readers about VLMs. We present a transparent, reproducible measurement of research trends across 26,104 accepted papers from CVPR, ICLR, and NeurIPS spanning 2023–2025.

name: From Pixels to Words – Towards Native Vision-Language
snippet: In this paper, we clarify these challenges and outline guiding principles for constructing native VLMs.

name: Understanding the visual knowledge of language models
snippet: You’ve likely heard that a picture is worth a thousand words, but can a large language model (LLM) get the picture if it’s never seen images before? As it turns out, language models that are trained purely on text have a solid understanding of the visual world.

name: A Review on Vision-Language-Based Approaches: Challenges and
snippet: In this paper, we present an extensive review of Vision-Language Pre-training (VLP), starting with an in-depth investigation of its basic ideas and the development of important models.

name: Unveiling Visual Perception in Language Models: An Attention
snippet: Recent advancements in Multimodal Large Language Mod-els (MLLMs) have demonstrated remarkable progress in visual understanding. This impressive leap raises a com-pelling question: how can language models, initially trained solely on linguistic data, effectively interpret and process visual content?

name: Visual cognition in multimodal large language models - Nature
snippet: This paper evaluates the current state of vision-based large language models in the domains of intuitive physics, causal reasoning and intuitive psychology.

name: Towards More Unified In-context Visual Understanding
snippet: To address this issue, we present a new ICL framework for visual understanding with multi-modal output enabled. First, we quantize and embed both text and visual prompt into a unified representational space, structured as interleaved in-context sequences.
